briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — aggressive pre-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for pre-spawn with stained water and pre frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, riprap, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: KVD: 'Three swimbait styles for different conditions: Bulkier hollow-body for slower presentations — great prespawn and stained water for extra vibration. Subtle finesse swimbaits (like Swimming Shiner) for clear water and tough bites on bright da...

Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal pre-spawn bass are aggressive and moving shallow. Expect fish in the 4-8ft range on secondary points and channel swings. Falling pressure pulls fish up from staging areas — target the first breakline off spawning flats.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
